scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Civil Judge (Senior Division)
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit. The Defendant is a person sui juris and can be sued. The suit property is situated within Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within the territorial limits of this Court. The Defendant resides within HSR Layout, Bengaluru, which is within the jurisdiction of this Court. This Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the suit pertains to immovable property situated within its local limits. This Court is the Court of the lowest grade competent to try the suit, and the suit value falls within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Court.

LIMITATION

2. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE AGREEMENT

3. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant for the purchase of residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft. The total consideration agreed upon was Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement stipulated that the Plaintiff would pay an advance amount and the balance against execution of the sale deed on or before 10.07.2024.

4. Pursuant to the agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS (UTR: HDFC20240110987654). The receipt for this payment is available and marked as Annexure B. The original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 is marked as Annexure A.

5. The balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 at the time of execution and registration of the sale deed. The Plaintiff has maintained sufficient funds to discharge this obligation and was prepared to tender the balance amount upon demand by the Defendant.

PLAINTIFF'S READINESS AND WILLINGNESS

6. The Plaintiff has always been and continues to be ready and willing to perform her part of the contract. The Plaintiff has arranged the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- and holds the funds available for payment against the execution of the sale deed. The Plaintiff has repeatedly offered to perform her obligations and requested the Defendant to fix a date for registration.

7. The Plaintiff is prepared to pay the balance consideration, bear the stamp duty, and registration charges as per the agreement terms. The Plaintiff has not committed any breach of the contract and has taken all necessary steps to ensure timely performance. The Plaintiff's conduct demonstrates continuous readiness and willingness to complete the transaction in accordance with the agreed terms.

DEFENDANT'S REFUSAL TO PERFORM

8. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the sale deed, claiming he had received a higher offer from a third party. This letter is marked as Annexure C. The Defendant's act constitutes a clear breach of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024.

9. To compel performance, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed as per the agreement. The legal notice with postal receipt is marked as Annexure D. The Defendant failed to comply with the notice and maintained his refusal to perform the contract. The Defendant's refusal has compelled the Plaintiff to approach this Court for relief.

LEGAL BASIS

10. The Plaintiff is entitled to specific performance under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, as the contract is for the transfer of immovable property and cannot be adequately compensated by damages. The enforcement is subject to Sections 11(2), 14, and 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963. The contract is valid and enforceable under Section 10 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

11. The Plaintiff satisfies the requirements of Section 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, as she has continuously performed and been ready and willing to perform the essential terms of the contract. The contract does not fall under the bars of Section 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, as it is not a contract for personal service or unsupervisable performance. The transfer of immovable property is specifically enforceable.

12. Time is not of the essence in this contract for immovable property unless expressly stipulated, and the Plaintiff's delay, if any, does not bar relief. Regarding registration, the agreement is relied upon for specific performance, and any reliance on Section 53A of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 is subject to applicable registration laws. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance if deemed fit by the Court. Relief for possession may be granted under Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

13. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant refused to perform his obligations under the Agreement to Sell by communicating his unwillingness to execute the sale deed. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has failed to perform his obligations despite the legal notice dated 20.06.2024. The suit is filed within the limitation period prescribed under Article 54 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

14. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court, as the property is situated in Bengaluru and the Defendant resides in Bengaluru. The Plaintiff has a continuing right to seek specific performance due to the Defendant's ongoing refusal to execute the sale deed.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

15. For the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction, the suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only), being the total consideration of the suit property. The court fee is paid under the Karnataka Court Fees Act, 1957. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the plaint.

16. The plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee as per the applicable laws. The valuation is based on the consideration amount agreed in the Agreement to Sell. The Plaintiff submits that the valuation is correct and sufficient for the Court to adjudicate the matter.

PRAYER

17. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
(a) Grant a decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;
(b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff for the suit property upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
(c) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff if not already in possession;
(d) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
(e) Award pre-suit interest if contract, usage, or substantive law permits, and award pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 CPC as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
(f) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(g) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and pr
